$note: Personal AI-spend routing policy. Drop into a Continue config, an Augment guideline, or a custom router loop. Signals are 0..1. Tier names align with router.js DEFAULT_CATALOG; vendor model names are illustrative and current as of 2026-05.

rules.rationale: Latency dominates; cost is per-keystroke. Local or in-IDE small models only.
Single-shot question, no codebase context. Anything fancier is wasted credits.
Sonnet handles single-file edits cleanly. Don't burn Opus for this.
Pattern-matching task. Haiku for boilerplate, Sonnet when tests are non-trivial.
Needs big context window + code retrieval. Sonnet+Augment usually beats raw frontier here.
Stakes are high; cost of a miss > cost of the call. Use extended thinking.
State-space search. This is the actual use case Opus exists for.
Prose + structure. Sonnet writes cleaner specs than frontier models in my testing.
Always free, always local, always fast. No exceptions.
Cheapest vision-capable tier; Gemini Flash is often the value pick.
Never leaves the machine. Quality trade-off accepted in exchange for privacy.
Deploys, db writes, irreversible commits. Always run the verify step from plan().

global_rules: Don't pay Opus for Sonnet work — if Sonnet would be indistinguishable, Sonnet wins. Cost ratio ~20x.
Don't pay Sonnet for Haiku work — boilerplate, formatting, simple edits are Haiku-grade. Cost ratio ~10x.
Frontier is for irreversibility, ambiguity, and high context. Everywhere else, frontier is waste.
Always route embeddings to a local model. There is no reason to pay for cloud embeddings.
When stakes >= 0.7, append a verify step using the same or one-tier-up model.
